response: the idea that people with learning disabilities cannot succeed in school is a harmful and outdated belief that has no basis in fact. learning disabilities do not mean a lack of ability or potential. many individuals with learning disabilities have gone on to achieve great things in their academic and professional lives. they may learn in different ways and require different support but this does not mean they are less capable. it is important to recognize and celebrate the strengths and abilities of all people regardless of their learning differences. by providing the right resources and encouragement everyone has the chance to thrive.
